title: conophallus muelleri
intro: Conophallus muelleri, also known as the false parasol, is a species of fungus in the genus Conophallus, characterized by its parasol-shaped cap and white gills. The medicinal properties of Conophallus muelleri include its potential to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi, as well as its ability to stimulate the immune system. The health benefits of this plant are still being researched, but it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which may help to prevent illnesses such as the common cold and flu. Conophallus muelleri can be prepared as a herbal supplement in the form of capsules or tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
